mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with accelerating housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in more than 60% of rolling 12-month windows, or the cross-sectional correlation between INDPRO/HOUST growth and SPX forward returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, a sustained increase in core consumer prices erodes real purchasing power and forces tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered episodes, or the predictive power of CPIAUCSL momentum vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between GDPC1 growth rates and SPX forward returns drops below 0.05, or forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are statistically indistinguishable from flat quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 additions in a single month, labor demand outpaces supply, triggering wage-push inflation and higher discount rates that pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where PAYEMS additions surpass 200,000, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.5 over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in residential investment (proxied by housing starts) and rising consumer credit balances indicate a shift from durable goods spending to debt servicing, which dampens aggregate demand growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative forward returns in more than 55% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a panel regression of SPX returns on HOUST and RSAFS changes yields a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: During supply-side bottlenecks, a divergence where industrial production growth decelerates while consumer prices accelerate signals stagflationary pressures that compress equity risk premiums and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change falls below 0.0% while CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.3%
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered stagflation episodes, or the cross-sectional predictive rank of the INDPRO-CPIAUCSL divergence falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
